task_completion.description: Log in to ManageEngine ServiceDesk Plus using the provided administrator credentials.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The login screen with 'administrator' entered in the username field, or the ServiceDesk Plus main dashboard visible after a successful login.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Admin or Settings section of ServiceDesk Plus.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Admin/Settings dashboard is visible, showing various configuration categories (e.g., General Settings, Security Settings).

task_completion.description: Open the specific configuration page for Security Settings or Attachment Settings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A settings page titled 'Security Settings', 'General Settings', or similar, displaying options related to file uploads, attachments, or restricted extensions.

task_completion.description: Enter the required file extensions ('exe', 'bat', 'cmd', 'vbs', 'sh') into the restricted/blocked attachments field.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text field or list explicitly showing the extensions 'exe', 'bat', 'cmd', 'vbs', and 'sh' added to a blocklist or restricted extensions list.

task_completion.description: Save the updated attachment security settings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save' button being clicked, or a success banner/message indicating that the settings have been saved successfully.

integrity.description: The agent configured the settings via the ServiceDesk Plus web interface, not via direct database manipulation or terminal scripts.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the web browser interacting with the ServiceDesk Plus UI, rather than terminal windows executing SQL or shell scripts.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual ManageEngine ServiceDesk Plus application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser URL bar shows the local ServiceDesk Plus address (typically localhost or a local IP on port 8080) and the UI matches ManageEngine ServiceDesk Plus.

integrity.description: The agent did not just type the extensions into a text editor or terminal to simulate completion.
integrity.visual_evidence: The extensions are entered into the actual web form fields of the ServiceDesk Plus application, not a generic text editor like Notepad or Nano.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent must log in using username 'administrator' and password 'administrator'. The configuration must explicitly block the following exact file extensions: 'exe', 'bat', 'cmd', 'vbs', 'sh'. The underlying database parameter being modified in the application is 'RestrictedExtensions'.
